label: Standard — tech review channel new upload

input.video_title: I Used Linux as My Only OS for 30 Days — Here's What Happened
input.what_it_covers: A daily-driver test of Linux Mint for productivity work, covering app compatibility, gaming, workflow changes, and whether it can genuinely replace Windows for a non-developer.
input.surprising_detail: The biggest productivity gain wasn't from the OS itself — it was from removing the constant Windows Update interruptions that the tester hadn't realized were costing nearly 45 minutes per week.
input.channel_tone: Curious, honest, slightly nerdy but accessible to non-technical viewers

input.video_title: 5 Stretches You Should Do Every Morning
input.what_it_covers: Five simple stretches that take under 10 minutes and target the areas most affected by desk work — neck, shoulders, hip flexors, lower back, and hamstrings.

label: European context — Italian food channel, upcoming tease

input.video_title: Why Real Carbonara Has No Cream (And What Romans Actually Use)
input.what_it_covers: The authentic Roman carbonara recipe using guanciale and pecorino, the history of how cream entered non-Italian versions, and a side-by-side taste comparison.
input.surprising_detail: The dish was likely invented by Italian charcoal workers (carbonari) as a calorie-dense field meal, not in a Roman restaurant as most food histories suggest.
input.channel_tone: Passionate about Italian food traditions, opinionated but friendly, respects both tradition and experimentation
input.specific_question: What's the most controversial carbonara ingredient you've seen someone use?
